---
title: "Phase 6 ‚Äî cross-mosaic summary"
subtitle: "Detection efficiency vs zodi √ó coverage √ó parameter choice across 10 skycells"
---

```{python}
#| echo: false
from pathlib import Path
import numpy as np, pandas as pd, matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from astropy.table import Table
REPO = Path("../..").resolve()
ALL = pd.read_csv(REPO / "output/detection/phase6/all_skycells.csv")
```

## What Phase 6 does

Roll up per-mosaic Phase 5 results from the skycell-driven Phase 4b
run (10 mosaics, 12 originally selected skycells less 2 in an HLWAS
coverage gap). For each mosaic we have:

- Mosaic median interior depth (from the coadd context array).
- Baseline detection efficiency (default `SourceCatalogStep` params).
- Kernel sweep (`kernel_fwhm` 2 / 3 / 5 / 7) and npixels sweep
  (`npixels` 9 / 16 / 25).

Plus Phase-1 metadata: ecliptic latitude, zodi band, romanisim sky
prediction.

## Per-pair summary

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| tbl-cap: "One row per mosaic. Depth is the median per-pixel depth in the interior. `mag_50_*_default` is the 50% completeness magnitude at `SourceCatalogStep` defaults; `*_n16` is the same at `npixels=16`."
ALL[["SKYCELL_ID", "skycell_name", "zodi_bin", "coverage_bin",
     "ECL_LAT_DEG", "SKY_E_PER_PIX_PER_S", "depth_median",
     "mag_50_stars_default", "mag_50_gals_default",
     "mag_50_stars_n16", "mag_50_gals_n16"]]
```

## 50%-completeness vs mosaic depth

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| fig-cap: "Mag at 50% star completeness (LEFT) and galaxy completeness (RIGHT) against median per-pixel mosaic depth. Points are colour-coded by zodi bin. Clear monotonic trend: deeper mosaics reach fainter magnitudes. Zero-depth mosaics have only ~2 L2 files each and most pixels lack any image coverage ‚Äî they sit ~1-2 mag shallower than depth-10 skycells."
from IPython.display import Image, display
display(Image(filename=str(REPO / "output/detection/phase6/mag50_vs_depth.png")))
```

## Parameter sweep ‚Äî does it hold up across depths?

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| fig-cap: "Œîmag_50 relative to default SourceCatalogStep settings. LEFT: stars ‚Äî `npixels=16` gives +0.5 mag at every depth (universal win; larger at lower depth). `kernel_fwhm=3` is marginal everywhere. RIGHT: galaxies ‚Äî `npixels=16` gains +0.05-0.15 mag; `kernel_fwhm=3` is zero or slightly negative. Phase 5c's finding from pair 11119 generalises across all depths and all zodi bands."
display(Image(filename=str(REPO / "output/detection/phase6/param_sweep_summary.png")))
```

## Default-parameter efficiency curves overlaid

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| fig-cap: "All mosaics' default efficiency curves overlaid, colour-coded by depth (dark = shallow, light = deep). After the Phase-1 tightening to `count_0p25 ‚â• 3`, every mosaic reaches depth ‚â• 2, so the curves form a clean family with smooth zodi + depth stratification ‚Äî no more depth-0 outliers."
display(Image(filename=str(REPO / "output/detection/phase6/baseline_all.png")))
```

## Per-skycell efficiency panels

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| fig-cap: "Per-skycell efficiency curves ‚Äî one row per skycell, stars (LEFT) and galaxies (RIGHT). Solid black = default `SourceCatalogStep`; dashed lines = `npixels` variants (9 red, 16 orange, 25 blue ‚Äî same as default) at `kernel_fwhm=2`. Each panel's row label shows the skycell's ecliptic latitude and per-pixel median depth."
display(Image(filename=str(REPO / "output/detection/phase6/per_skycell_panels.png")))
```

## Takeaways

1. **`npixels=16` is a universal win** (+0.5 mag star completeness,
   +0.1 mag galaxy) at every depth and zodi band tested. The
   per-mosaic cost is zero ‚Äî it just re-runs `SourceCatalogStep`.
2. **Depth drives completeness** much more than zodi. In the depth
   ‚â•6 regime, zodi variation contributes ~0.1-0.3 mag; the depth
   spread from 0 ‚Üí 10 contributes ~2 mag.
3. **The `kernel_fwhm=2` default is correct** across the board. Wider
   kernels hurt at high depth (blending), break even at low depth.
4. **Depth-0 ("coverage gap") skycells** give very noisy curves ‚Äî not
   useful for characterising the HLWAS-nominal detection regime.
   Worth redesigning Phase 1 to pick "low coverage" at 1-3 exposures
   within 0.25¬∞ (rather than ‚â§0) so the low-depth tier still has
   partial mosaic coverage.

## Checkpoint

Phase 6 deliverable. The skycell-driven design works end-to-end at
12-skycell scale. Parameter recommendations from pair 11119
(Phase 5c) generalise to the full sample.
